digest.component_or_area: release pipeline build-once + Hatchling provenance hook
propositions: The release wheel built via the default sdist-to-wheel path bakes COMMIT = None because the sdist has no .git, so the published wheel lacks a real source SHA while only the sdist keeps it.
The release artifact and the CI-tested code come from different build paths, and the real artifact is only smoke-tested post-publication when PyPI immutability makes recovery costly.
Adopt a build-once pattern: build wheel and sdist once, record SHA-256, test the exact artifacts in clean jobs, and publish the same bytes after hash verification with no rebuild.

Fix hatch_build.py initialize with four-step commit precedence: REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT env var, then preserve existing non-null COMMIT (for install-from-sdist), then git rev-parse --short HEAD, else None.
The hook must fail-fast only when REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT is set but empty/unresolvable, while an unset env var degrades gracefully to git for developer pip install .

Canonical commit format is the short SHA to keep the baked value consistent with signing.py's live-git value, and existing tests must add coverage for the previously-untested wheel-from-sdist COMMIT = None case.
